entries.definition:
كتاب النون

نوي

(نوس) النون والواو والسين أصلٌ يدلُّ على اضطرابٍ وتذبذُب. وناسَ الشَّيءُ: تذَبْذَب، ينُوس. وسمِّي أبو نُوَاسٍ لذُؤَابتينِ له كانَتَا تنوسانِ. ويقولون: نُسْت الإبلَ: سُقْتُها.

n–w–s human beings, to increase, to shake; to be blown about,
to drive an animal; to slacken, to hang down; cobweb. In addition
to deriving ناس nās from this root (to move about), philologists derive
it also from roots أُنْس ʾ–n–s (to be sociable) and ن/س/ي n–s–y (to
be forgetful). Of this root, only ناس nās occurs 240 times in the
Qur’an.

النَّاس ʾal-nās [with the def. art., coll. n.] 1 (contrasted to jinn
(q.v.) and angels) humankind (22:75) اللَّهُ يَصْطَفِي مِنَ الْمَلَائِكَةِ رُسُلًا وَمِنَ
النَّاسِ God chooses messengers from angels and humankind;
(114:6) مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ [whither they be] from among the jinn or
humankind; * الناس name of Sura 114, Meccan sura, so-named
because of the references throughout to ‘People’ 2 people (4:1)
يَاأَيُّهَا النَّاسُ اتَّقُوا رَبَّكُمُ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ مِنْ نَفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ people, be mindful of your
Lord, who created you from a single soul 3 a crowd (7:116) سَحَرُوا
أَعْيُنَ النَّاسِ وَاسْتَرْهَبُوهُمْ they cast a spell on the eyes of the crowds and
struck fear into them 4 those in question (12:46) لَعَلِّي أَرْجِعُ إِلَى النَّاسِ
that I might return [with the solution] to those who have sent me
5 others (4:142) وَإِذَا قَامُوا إِلَى الصَّلَاةِ قَامُوا كُسَالَىٰ يُرَاءُونَ النَّاسَ when they
stand up to pray, they do so sluggishly, hypocritically [for show]
in order to impress others 6 (contrasted to الناس) a particular
group (3:174) الَّذِينَ قَالَ لَهُمُ النَّاسُ إِنَّ النَّاسَ قَدْ جَمَعُوا لَكُمْ those who were
told by those around them that their enemy had amassed [a big
army] against them 7 every one, all and sundry (2:83) وَقُولُوا لِلنَّاسِ
حُسْنًا and speak words of kindness to everyone 8 those endowed
with what ʾal-Rāghib calls the essence of humanity, the good, the
humane (4:54) أَمْ يَحْسُدُونَ النَّاسَ عَلَىٰ مَا آتَاهُمُ اللَّهُ مِنْ فَضْلِهِ do they envy
those to whom God has given some of His grace?!
